publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-02-22 18:46:30 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes correspondence, published material and some manuscripts.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Mehrotra is a 20th century modernist poet of India, an editor, translator and literary critic. Along with Adil Jussawalla, Mehrotra formed the Clearing House, which published the works of leading figures such as Arun Kolatkar, Gieve Patel, and Dilip Chitre, among others. The "Bombay Poets" were not strictly confined to Bombay/Mumbai, but included figures such as AK Ramanujan, who lived in the United States.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, published material and some manuscripts.

emph.ref: Series I. Arvind Krishna Mehrotra Manuscripts, Drafts, Personal Materials and Reviews
Series III. Other Authors and Miscellaneous

list.head: Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections.
list.item: de Souza, Eunice. Learn from the Almond Leaf, 2016. Signed.
Mehrotra, Arvind Krishna. Bharatmata: a prayer, 1967. Offprint from Poetry India.
Kolatkar, Arun. The Boatride, circa 1960s. Signed self-made book, with experimental paper treatment.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Arvind Krishna Mehrotra Papers, #8511. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Related material located in collection #8519, Adil Jussawalla papers.

did.unittitle: Series I. Arvind Krishna Mehrotra Manuscripts, Drafts, Personal Materials and Reviews

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Agha Shahid Ali, letters from Adil (new writing in India), Tornado #5 (expenses), letters from US Poets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In Century (1966), Poetry India (1967), Manhattan Review(1966-1967)

did.unittitle: Contributions to other US, UK, and Indian magazines

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes NPW/Kruti and Tornado #4, Bombay Duck

did.unittitle: Songs of Kabir MSS and drafts
History of Indian Literature in English file
History of Indian Literature in English initial stages
History of Indian Literature in English correspondence
Looking for A.K. Ramanujan clippings, notes and drafts
The Transfiguring Places drafts of poems
MSS drafts of poems that first appear under New Poems in Collected Poems
Copies of The Nation, MPT (17), Romanian Bulletin, all with AK Mehrotra poems or translations; Penguin News with announcement of Adil Jussawalla's "New Writing in India".
The Picador Book of Modern Indian Literature publishing information, photocopy with corrections of "The Emperor Has No Clothes".
MSS of the Absent Traveller with AK Ramanujan's corrections/suggestions in pencil, with letters from Selby and Ramanujan
MSS of the Absent Traveller, notes and drafts, with letter from Selby
The Absent Traveller notes and drafts
Mehrotra's Curriculum Vitae, book selection photocopies, materials for a grant/fellowship that never came through
The Transfiguring Places proofs, drafts, letters to Ravi Dayal
Clippings and notes related to History of Indian Literature in English

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Prathibha Nandakumar letter and photocopies of AK Ramanujan letters/poems to her.

did.unittitle: Articles and reviews received from Adil Jussawalla, including letters from Jussawalla
AK Mehrortra early chapbooks: "Woodcuts on Paper" and "Pomes, Poemes, Poemas"
List of early publications, early letters to publishers, translations of poems in German
Three early Mehrortra essays, one unpublished;, international writing program Iowa presentations and materials; Center for New Performing Arts Iowa publications
Kabir beginnings, including signed copy of Robert Bly's chapbook "The Fish in the Water is Not Thirsty (1971), and poster for the Indian edition of "Songs of Kabir"
"All to Remember", unpublished typescript of Amit Rai (1948-1966) collected poems, prose, and letters edited by Mehrortra.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Rai was one of the three editors of Damn You.

did.unittitle: Making books, including final MSS of Distance to Statute Miles
History of Indian Literature in English
After Maluk MS, Paper Aeroplane, Poems from the goof surrealist MS, Partial recall essay, Nirala MS, letters from Helsa, Gulam Sheikh, the Yaddo file, GRE scores

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of Genesis, Mehrotra's first "book," produced when he was 17 (one bound, one unbound); small notebook with Siva on cover—poems, pieces, doodles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flow, Indian Mountain and Other Poems, The Road to Black Mountain

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including Row With Your Hair, Troll Songs, Zuk, Cold Mountain Press, Nights of Naomi, Let the Wild Rumpus Start, Wondering Where You Are, Westbeth Poetry

did.unittitle: "The Dolphin with the Revolver in its Teeth", "Shaded Room" (1), "Quark" (1967), "Maelstro" (1967), "Earthword" (1971), "Bear"
"The Beauties of Travel", "The Poor Devil Eire", "Llamas Allmanac", "Margins" (1975)
Journal of South Asian Literature (1974), Mahfil
"The Nation", with Grace Shulman's poems and correspondence
British and US little magazines, including "Intrepid" special issue on India
TriQuarterly 37: Going to Heaven, with a letter from Elliott Anderson
"Town Poems" by Ashok Chopra, an Ezra-Fakir publications, and single copy of Ezra #2
"Damn You" issues 1-6 and "An Odd Thing" by Anil Saari, a Damn You press publications
"Three" by Bogomil Gjuzel, translated by Mehrotra, Ezra-Fakir press
"Ezra" 1, 3-5; Fakir; "Bharatmata"; "Concrete poetry" loose sheets; twelve poems from the "Good Surrealist"; Clearing House cards and flyers
Miscellaneous programs and small press books
Series III. Other Authors and Miscellaneous
Agha Shahid Ali, Keki Daruwalla, Dilip Chitre clippings and letters
Dom Moraes, Arun Kolatkar, and notes on Jejuri
Jayanta Mahapatra poems, clippings, and letters
Nissim Ezekiel xeroxes, reviews, Edinburgh interlude, note on Ezekiel
Arun Kolatkar unpublished MSS of his novel 'Royan'
"Nile" poems by Tomaz Salamun, autographed typescript

Copy of Adil Jussawalla "Land's End" with his corrections and letter; photocopies of Jussawalla's "Trying to Say Goodbye" and "The Right Kind of Dog"; copies of Dilip Chitre chapbooks; Mehrotra's notes on translation and unpublished interview with Vijay Kumar

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In English, an unsewn manuscript signature of notes, poems, and sketches (1960s?); in Marathi, two manuscript signatures of poetry, hand-written and water-stained; Signed typescript of Jejuri in English, 1974.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including letters from Ved Mehra and Palash Krishna Mehrotra; letter from Arun Kolatkar's nephew

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including letters from Adil, Manohar, Jeet, Ranjit, Allan, Pankaj (postcard about first impressions of the US)

did.unittitle: Letters from writers: Adil Jussawalla, Shetty, Jayanta, Daniel Weissbort, Timothy Hyman, and others
Letters from friends and academics, including Bruce King, Martha Selby, and others
Letters from Adil, Shetty, Allan Sealy, Weissbort and others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three photocopied letters from Rukum Advani of OUP soliciting permission on any terms to publish Arun Kolatkar's work.
